input.prompt:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to build a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server using stdio transport. They need:

1. A tool that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI)
2. A prompt template for summarization
3. Logging that doesn't interfere with stdio JSON-RPC protocol

Let me first check if there's anything in the current working directory, then create the project.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
</member>
        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute">
            <summary>
            Used to indicate that a method should be considered an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/>.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This attribute is applied to methods that should be exposed as prompts in the Model Context Protocol. When a class
            containing methods marked with this attribute is registered with McpServerBuilderExtensions,
            these methods become available as prompts that can be called by MCP clients.
            </para>
            <para>
--
        </member>
        <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute.#ctor">
            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute"/> class.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute.Name">
            <summary>Gets or sets the name of the prompt.</summary>
            <remarks>If <see langword="null"/>, the method name is used.</remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute.Title">
            <summary>Gets or sets the title of the prompt.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute.IconSource">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the source URI for the prompt's icon.
            </summary>
---
        </member>
        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute">
            <summary>
            Indicates that a method should be considered an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This attribute is applied to methods that should be exposed as tools in the Model Context Protocol. When a class
            containing methods marked with this attribute is registered with McpServerBuilderExtensions,
            these methods become available as tools that can be called by MCP clients.
            </para>
            <para>
--
        </member>
        <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.#ctor">
            <summary>
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute"/> class.

arguments.command:
# Check how the McpServerPrompt method return type works - can it just return string or IEnumerable<PromptMessage>?
grep -B2 -A15 'method should return' ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.0.0/lib/net8.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml | head -40
echo "==="
# Also check TextContentBlock constructor
grep -B1 -A10 'TextContentBlock' ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.0.0/lib/net8.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml | head -30

# Check how the McpServerPrompt method return type works - can it just return string or IEnumerable<PromptMessage>?
grep -B2 -A15 'method should return' ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.0.0/lib/net8.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml | head -40
echo "==="
# Also check TextContentBlock constructor
grep -B1 -A10 'TextContentBlock' ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.0.0/lib/net8.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml | head -30

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
===
            The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> class is a fundamental type in the MCP that can represent different forms of content
            based on the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Type"/> property. Derived types like <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock"/>, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock"/>,
            and <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmbeddedResourceBlock"/> provide the type-specific content.
            </para>
            <para>
            This class is used throughout the MCP for representing content in messages, tool responses,
            and other communication between clients and servers.
            </para>
            <para>
            See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for more details.
            </para>
            </remarks>
--
        </member>
        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock">
            <summary>Represents text provided to or from an LLM.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock.Type">
            <inheritdoc/>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock.Text">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the text content of the message.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock.ToString">
            <inheritdoc/>
        </member>
        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock">
            <summary>Represents an image provided to or from an LLM.</summary>
<exited with exit code 0>
